name: Samson
description: Samson, a legendary Israelite warrior and judge, is renowned for his incredible strength, which was bestowed upon him by God under the condition that he never cut his hair. Born to Manoah and his wife in the tribe of Dan, Samson's birth was foretold by an angel, marking him as a Nazirite from birth, dedicated to God's service. His strength manifested early, allowing him to perform feats such as tearing a lion apart with his bare hands. Samson's life was marked by conflicts with the Philistines, who controlled the region. He used his strength to harass and fight them, often single-handedly defeating large numbers of Philistine warriors. His exploits include killing a thousand men with the jawbone of an ass and escaping the city of Gaza by carrying its gates away. Samson's downfall came through his relationship with Delilah, a Philistine woman who was bribed to discover the source of his strength. After repeated attempts, Delilah finally tricked Samson into revealing that his strength resided in his uncut hair. While he slept, she had his hair cut, and the Philistines captured him, blinded him, and forced him to grind grain in prison. In his final act of defiance, Samson prayed to God for strength one last time. While at a Philistine festival dedicated to their god Dagon, Samson was brought out to be mocked. He asked to be positioned between the two central pillars of the temple. With renewed strength, he pushed against the pillars, causing the temple to collapse, killing himself and thousands of Philistines. Samson's story is a testament to the power of faith, the consequences of disobedience, and the potential for redemption, even in the face of adversity. He remains a symbol of strength and resistance in Jewish tradition.
subtitle: The Strongest Man of Israel
achievements: Tore a lion apart with his bare hands
Killed thirty Philistines to pay a debt
Slaughtered a thousand men with a donkey's jawbone
Carried away the gates of Gaza
Destroyed the temple of Dagon, killing thousands

keyMyths.title: Birth of Samson
keyMyths.description: An angel appeared to Manoah's barren wife and foretold the birth of a son, Samson, who would begin to deliver Israel from the Philistines. He was to be a Nazirite from birth, abstaining from wine, strong drink, and cutting his hair. This divine announcement set the stage for Samson's extraordinary life.

keyMyths.title: Samson and the Lion
keyMyths.description: While traveling to Timnah, Samson was attacked by a young lion. The Spirit of the Lord came upon him, and he tore the lion apart with his bare hands as easily as one would tear a young goat. This event marked the beginning of Samson's legendary feats of strength.

keyMyths.title: Samson's Final Act
keyMyths.description: Imprisoned and blinded by the Philistines, Samson was brought to the temple of Dagon for their amusement. He prayed to God for strength and, positioning himself between the two central pillars, pushed them down, collapsing the temple and killing himself and thousands of Philistines. This was his ultimate act of vengeance and sacrifice.

sources.author: Various Authors
sources.description: The primary source for the story of Samson in the Hebrew Bible.
sources.text: The primary source for the story of Samson in the Hebrew Bible.

sources.author: Josephus
sources.description: A historical account that retells and interprets the biblical narrative, including the story of Samson.
sources.text: A historical account that retells and interprets the biblical narrative, including the story of Samson.
